title: The second inverse two-point DFT coordinate recovers the second input

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Applying the inverse two-point DFT second-coordinate formula to the DC and Nyquist modes recovers the second signal entry.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The second inverse coordinate equals u1.

declarations.name: dft2DC
declarations.statement: def dft2DC (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 + u1

declarations.name: dft2Nyquist
declarations.statement: def dft2Nyquist (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 - u1

declarations.name: idft2Second
declarations.statement: def idft2Second (low high : Real) : Real := (low - high) / 2

declarations.name: idft2Second_dft2
declarations.statement: theorem idft2Second_dft2 (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2Second (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u1

tasks.input: Translate recovery of the second coordinate by the inverse two-point DFT into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem idft2Second_dft2 (u0 u1 : Real) : idft2Second (dft2DC u0 u1) (dft2Nyquist u0 u1) = u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the transform definitions and simplifying.
tasks.target: Unfold idft2Second, dft2DC, and dft2Nyquist, then use ring.
